actionUnits.condition: Initialization coverage: MonoBehaviour_0 (m_Script guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) is the Fire script. On Awake, it assigns pSystem from mainEffectGo (fileID 1849464941111748095) and sets timeToOn = Random.Range(timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60). BoxCollider present (enabled=1, isTrigger=0, size 1.5x2.5x0.25). This step allows Awake/first Update to run with current serialized state: isOn=0, shouldTurnOn=0, neighbors=[], onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, maxHitInterval=2.

Turn-on propagation path: Provide at least 2 neighboring Fire components within maxNeighborDistance (4) so onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold (2). Neighbors must call NotifyNeighborState(true) on this instance (happens automatically when they turn on and notify). With shouldTurnOn=true, Update accumulates timeToOnDelta until >= timeToOn (random 40-60). If any neighbor is below this fire by > minNeighborDownDistance=3 on Y, downMultiplier=2 speeds accumulation. Expected outcome: this.isOn becomes true and UpdateFireState(true) runs, playing particles and notifying neighbors; FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1) is invoked.

Manual trigger we want to test: WaterHit to turn fire off. Preconditions: fire is currently ON (from previous step). During this window, ensure IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) is invoked on this component repeatedly with intervals < maxHitInterval (2s) so currentHitInterval never exceeds 2s until currentHitTime reaches totalHitTime (5s). Expected outcome: fire turns off (isOn=false), shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff resets, UpdateFireState(true) runs stopping all particles and notifies neighbors; FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1) is invoked.

Edge case: cancel turning-off if hits are too sparse. Preconditions: fire is ON. Call WaterHit once to set isTurningOff=true, then do not call WaterHit again for >= maxHitInterval (2s). Expected outcome: after currentHitInterval >= 2s, Update cancels turning-off (isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0) and fire remains ON.
